item.title: Loved ones of Australian woman question backpacker Tobias Pick's manslaughter sentence
item.description: In 2020, a German backpacker strangled a Tasmanian sex worker, then fled with her mobile phone and cash. His version of events was accepted by an Australian court, which jailed him for manslaughter. Now, German authorities are re-examining the case.

item.title: Fly larvae turns Riverland food and farm waste into animal feed
item.description: A regional South Australian couple's fly larvae farm is using discarded produce to make a valuable food source.

item.title: UAE minister Reem Al Hashimy describes Iran's attacks as 'almost unhinged', tells ABC they surprised her
item.description: Iran's actions in attacking neighbouring countries were unprecedented, "almost unhinged" and irrational, according to a senior United Arab Emirates minister.

item.title: Floodwaters spread into Longreach's outskirts with peak expected on Sunday
item.description: The swollen Thomson River has carried floodwaters closer to homes with levels forecast to continue rising.

item.title: Inside Lainey Wilson's non‑stop rise through tours, Netflix doco, Stagecoach and new single
item.description: Arena tours, feature films and a new single — here's how one of America's biggest stars keeps climbing.

item.title: Eight players on Tony Popovic's radar to make the Socceroos 26-man 2026 World Cup squad
item.description: Tony Popovic wants his players to show "toughness" as all Socceroos sides have done in the past, but he wants them to shed the underdog tag. These players could help Australia do that at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: New discovery of rare Lord Howe Island stick insect colonies
item.description: Once so common they were used as bait, Lord Howe Island stick insects are making a comeback from the brink of extinction.

item.title: Cracks appear in Runit Dome amid sea level rise in Marshall Islands
item.description: Cracks have appeared in a concrete dome holding 120,000 tonnes of contaminated soil and debris from Cold War-era nuclear bomb tests.

item.title: If you feel like the world is spinning too quickly, look at who's setting the pace
item.description: Around the world, things are moving at a dizzying speed, and the pace is being set from Washington. Some people claim this is a deliberate strategy to overwhelm us, and that it comes with consequences.

item.title: Barnaby Joyce regrets not doing more to shore up fuel stockpile, amid inflation fears
item.description: One Nation MP Barnaby Joyce says he regrets not doing more when he was in government to secure fuel reserves, as Australia grapples with supply shortages triggered by war in the Middle East.

item.title: Perth family locked in battle with NDIS over care for disabled son
item.description: The parents of a disabled teenager have lifted the lid on the toll lobbying the NDIS for adequate support has taken on their lives, saying they feel exhausted and let down.

item.title: Motörhead guitarist Phil Campbell dies aged 64
item.description: In a statement on Instagram, his family says he "passed away peacefully following a long and courageous battle in intensive care after a complex major operation".

item.title: Petracca dominates as Suns cruise against Eagles, Demons win thriller against Saints
item.description: New recruit Christian Petracca is sensational again as Gold Coast smashes West Coast, after Melbourne completes a thrilling and memorable MCG win over St Kilda.

item.title: What is breastfeeding aversion and how can you manage it?
item.description: Anger, dread, anxiety, guilt, worry and disconnect are just some of the feelings described by women who experience breastfeeding aversion response.

item.title: Explosion targets Iranian space facility in Tehran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: France wins back-to-back Six Nations Championships, clinching the title with a 48-46 win over England
item.description: After five rounds of matches, the Six Nations Championship comes down to a single penalty in the 83rd minute of the final game — and France emerges as back-to-back champions.

item.title: Fire at UAE oil hub
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Half their salary cap gone': Knights count cost of win over Manly
item.description: Kalyn Ponga and Dylan Brown are both forced from the field in the first half as the Newcastle Knights ran riot against the Sea Eagles at Brookvale Oval, before the Dolphins storm back from 14-0 down to beat the Titans and become the first Queensland team to win a game this season.

item.title: North-west town the first in Victoria to run out of fuel
item.description: Robinvale, located about 70 kilometres east from Mildura, ran out of fuel on Saturday evening.

item.title: Aftermath of Israeli attack on medical centre in Lebanon
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Raissa Butkowski to contest Farrer for Liberals after Sussan Ley's resignation
item.description: Raissa Butkowski has been selected as the Liberal candidate for the seat of Farrer.

item.title: Three fishermen missing off Beachport along SA's Limestone Coast
item.description: Emergency crews are responding after three fishermen were declared missing at Beachport in South Australia's south-east.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Japan dominates Philippines to cruise into semifinals and delight diaspora
item.description: Sayoko had never played football, but the Japan women's side inspired her to take up the game. And the Nadeshiko lived up to the hype at Stadium Australia, defeating the Philippines 7-0 on an afternoon of colour and national pride.

item.title: This week in history quiz: COVID cruise ships, Neighbours launches and walking in space
item.description: This week in years past saw the first ever spacewalk, the Harbour Bridge opening and more importantly the premiere of Neighbours. Test your memory.

item.title: These musicians stole our hearts on the first season of The Piano, so what are they up to now?
item.description: In the first season of The Piano, nobody quite knew what to expect. But what happens after you get the chance to play for a national audience? We catch up with some of the pianists to find out how music continues to shape their lives.

item.title: What the government knew about SA’s toxic algal bloom and what it told the public
item.description: The government knew a harmful toxin had been found in its algal bloom, but took months to update its health advice.

item.title: Doctors want better protection for workers exposed to workplace chemicals labelled 'silent killers'
item.description: Under the proposed changes, there would be a five-fold reduction in the exposure limit for benzene, chlorine exposure would be reduced ten-fold, and silica exposure would be halved.

item.title: Australia's refugee policy is tested as Iran's humanitarian crisis expands
item.description: Does the same principle that led Australia to support Israel and the US in Iran also extend to offering more humanitarian assistance?

item.title: What is lifestyle creep? Here's why it happens and how to avoid it
item.description: If you tend to splurge on more indulgences any time you receive a pay rise, you may be suffering from lifestyle creep — and it could have a longstanding effect on your finances.

item.title: One Nation MP Barnaby Joyce predicts party will 'poll number one' in many seats at SA election
item.description: Federal MP Barnaby Joyce is predicting a big primary vote for One Nation at this Saturday's South Australian election, but lead candidate Cory Bernardi would not be drawn on how many lower house seats he hoped the party would win.

item.title: Study of Brisbane family helps researchers uncover genetic mutation linked to ulcerative colitis
item.description: Researchers have used advanced genetic sequencing on a Brisbane family to identify a gene mutation linked to ulcerative colitis, a type of inflammatory bowel condition, sparking hopes of new treatments.

item.title: Fuel rationing a chance in Australia if war continues to trim global oil supplies, experts say
item.description: Experts say Australians should prepare for the possibility of widespread fuel rationing if the war in the Middle East is prolonged.

item.title: Nepali security guard killed in Abu Dhabi shows risks faced by 'trapped' migrant workers
item.description: The killing of a Nepali security guard in Abu Dhabi highlights the growing risks faced by millions of migrant workers across the Gulf as the conflict between Iran, Israel and the United States escalates.

item.title: Fifth member of Iranian women's soccer team leaves Australia
item.description: A fifth member of the Iranian women's football team withdrew her asylum claim and left the country overnight, the federal government says.

item.title: Popular mango farm forced to sell as compliance measures pressure producers
item.description: Mango industry representatives say addressing mounting compliance requirements is like "trying to turn back the Titanic".

item.title: ASX slips as war rattles global stocks, RBA rate hike looms — as it happened
item.description: Global investors are still rotating out of shares as the Iranian conflict continues and rising oil prices stoke inflation fears, but locally the ASX has finished down just 0.4pc. Look back at how the trading session unfolded.

item.title: Three New South Wales fishermen found dead off SA's Beachport
item.description: SA police say the alarm was raised yesterday afternoon, when the three men did not return to shore at Beachport in the state's south-east. A local fisher said he tried to warn the men before they left that conditions were rough.

item.title: Australia will not send navy ship to Strait of Hormuz, minister says — as it happened
item.description: Australia will not be sending a navy ship to the Strait of Hormuz to protect oil-carrying vessels from Iranian attacks, the transport minister says.

item.title: UEFA says intercontinental football champions match between Argentina and Spain is off due to Middle East war
item.description: A meeting of men's football's South American and European champions — the Finalissima — featuring superstars Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal, has been called off due to security concerns amid the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Impassioned plea from Tasmania's top cop no match for political realities on gun ownership caps
item.description: Gun law reform is always fraught, especially in Tasmania. An impassioned plea from the state's top cop hasn't been enough to sway the government on gun ownership caps, but opposition and crossbench MPs may be able to pass the limits into law.

item.title: Rose Byrne, Jacob Elordi miss out at Oscars as One Battle After Another dominates — as it happened
item.description: Australians Rose Byrne and Jacob Elordi miss out on their first Oscars, as Michael B Jordan gets a standing ovation for winning Best Actor and One Battle After Another sweeps the field.

item.title: Queensland arts minister approved name for Glasshouse Theatre before public vote opened, documents show
item.description: Documents released under right to information show Queensland's arts minister was "set" on the name in January, months before the community was invited to have their say.

item.title: Homeless Indigenous elder died after years on 'priority' WA public housing waitlist
item.description: The Wongatha man from WA's Goldfields died in Kalgoorlie-Boulder, about 600km east of Perth, on February 6 after battling emphysema and lung cancer.

item.title: Oscars 2026 red carpet: Rose Byrne, Nicole Kidman represent Australia on Academy Awards red carpet
item.description: Before the ceremony of the 98th Oscars is the spectacle that is the red carpet. See all the most striking looks here.

item.title: Kimberley farmers face three-year wait for new weather radar
item.description: Farmers in WA's north typically rely on real-time radar data for planting purposes during the wet season.

item.title: Fish price spike, supply issues months after WA's demersal fishing ban
item.description: The ABC has spoken to six fishmongers who have had to increase prices after the WA government's demersal fishing ban, with consumers set to pay 10 to 40 per cent more for most species this Easter.

item.title: Oscars 2026 winners list: Michael B Jordan takes Best Actor, Jessie Buckley best actress
item.description: Here's the updated winners list as the ceremony takes place.

item.title: Family's quest for sensory-safe playgrounds following daughter's tragic death at The Grotto lookout
item.description: The family of a six-year-old girl with autism who fell from the cliffs at a popular NSW South Coast lookout a year ago want to see more inclusive public spaces.

item.title: Road users encounter stray cattle on rural roads in the Western Australia's Great Southern.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Ongoing stray livestock issue across WA shires leaving impacted farmers frustrated
item.description: A Great Southern farmer joins growing calls for stronger powers and greater accountability to address wandering livestock on rural properties and roads.

item.title: Corellas wreak havoc in Bunbury prompting council's call to report sightings
item.description: Little corellas are stripping trees, chewing on infrastructure and generally being a noisy nuisance in Bunbury.

item.title: Aftermath of an Israeli strike on a medical centre in Lebanon
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australian author Craig Silvey faces further child exploitation charges
item.description: Popular Australian author Craig Silvey is charged with two more child exploitation offences, including allegedly producing child exploitation material.

item.title: Victorian government agrees to $125 million COVID hotel quarantine class action settlement
item.description: The state government has agreed to a $125 million settlement, which has to be approved by the Supreme Court.

item.title: Court told mother faked son's cancer diagnosis to cover her overspending
item.description: An Adelaide mother who faked her six-year-old son's cancer diagnosis to deceitfully raise money was "living the life of the rich and famous" despite serious financial struggles, a court has heard.

item.title: Drone-related incident sparks fire at Dubai Airport fuel tank
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Gone in 15 seconds: thousands of Victorian cars stolen using high-tech devices
item.description: Police say criminals are able to steal certain push-button start models within 15 seconds, using key reprogramming devices bought online.

item.title: Invasive rice grass removed from Tasmanian wetland
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: David Speirs looking to overcome drugs sentence and return to South Australian parliament
item.description: One-time Liberal opposition leader David Speirs is attempting a political comeback, but even he concedes it's a long shot after a very public downfall where he was convicted of supplying cocaine just over 10 months ago.

item.title: Three new rock monitor lizard species discovered in Far North Queensland savanna
item.description: The unexpected findings in the savanna country of north Queensland have changed scientists' understanding of rock monitors, but there are concerns the new species could be vulnerable to the pet trade.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands and Jackie O network slapped with new licence conditions
item.description: The media watchdog imposes fresh conditions on KIIS FM owner ARN Media, in response to repeated breaches by controversial radio show The Kyle & Jackie O Show.

item.title: Oscars 2026 key moments: Australians miss out across all major categories
item.description: Australians have missed out in every key category at the 98th Academy Awards. Michael B Jordan received a standing ovation when named Best Actor for Sinners. And Conan O'Brien also "wins". Here are your Oscars key moments.

item.title: Four slow-cooker meals for cooler autumn evenings
item.description: We've pulled together four lovely recipes that make weeknight meals a little easier (and quite budget friendly, too).

item.title: Magpies to challenge Scott Pendlebury's AFL ban, Swans star Errol Gulden out for four months
item.description: Collingwood decides if it will contest Scott Pendlebury's one-match suspension at the AFL tribunal, as the veteran midfielder faces a ban for the first time in his 427-match career.

item.title: Every NSW waterway contaminated with microplastics, study finds
item.description: A NSW government study finds microplastics in all 120 estuaries sampled, with severe pollution in about 19 per cent of rivers and estuaries.

item.title: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas doubles down on algal bloom health messaging
item.description: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas has doubled down on public health messaging about the state's algal bloom crisis saying the word "toxic" could also be used for alcohol. It follows questions raised by the ABC's Four Corner's program about the time frame in delivering health advice on the bloom.

item.title: Homicide squad investigating death of man in Jung in western Victoria
item.description: Homicide squad detectives are investigating the death of a man whose body was found in the western Victorian town of Jung.

item.title: AFL referred to antisemitism royal commission after opening round speech controversy
item.description: The club says it takes full responsibility for a script change that removed references to the Jewish community during an opening round tribute to Bondi victims.

item.title: Calls for more government action to help prevent youth homelessness in WA
item.description: A downward spiral led Sully into homelessness — now he's part of a coalition dedicated to getting young people off the streets and into accommodation.

item.title: Fuel shortages spark WA farmers' ire amid claims city is getting priority access
item.description: Farmers welcome a rule change that allows road trains to deliver more fuel to regional areas, but say it does little to address the overall supply problem.

item.title: Matildas duo Hayley Raso and Steph Catley fit to play China in Asian Cup semifinal
item.description: Hayley Raso and Steph Catley are available for selection, but it remains to be seen what role they will have against China in Perth.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Return of the mums, retired shooter winds back the clock and Kiwis make hot start at Firebirds
item.description: The world's best goal keeper has sparked an important conversation about postpartum depression in her return to the Super Netball league.

item.title: Should data centres fund their own power generation?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Ex-Lib leader in SA seeking election comeback despite drug conviction
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Germany says Iran war 'not a matter for NATO', as Trump demands Strait of Hormuz help
item.description: Donald Trump tells US allies to "get involved quickly" in unblocking the Strait of Hormuz shipping channel, while warning members of NATO the alliance faces a "very bad" future if they do not do their bit.

item.title: White House chief of staff Susie Wiles diagnosed with breast cancer
item.description: US President Donald Trump announces her diagnosis on Truth Social, saying her prognosis "is excellent".

item.title: Iranian women's football team begin journey home after five withdraw asylum claims in Australia
item.description: The Iranian women's football team leave Malaysia for the Middle East after five players withdraw their claims for asylum in Australia.

item.title: The world still interprets war through an American lens
item.description: For much of the past century, the world has learned to interpret war through categories shaped by American power.

item.title: A DNA test, a match and a decades-old IVF mix-up
item.description: When Sasha Szafranski matched with a stranger on a genealogy website, she uncovered an IVF mix-up that had been hidden for 30 years.

item.title: Tasmanian duck hunters blame protesters for burning shooting shelters at Moulting Lagoon
item.description: Tasmanian duck hunters believe their shooting shelters, known as hides, are being burnt down by protesters, with 80 destroyed in the past four years.

item.title: With oil prices soaring, can Venezuela's vast supplies fill the gap?
item.description: Foreign Correspondent goes inside Venezuela's oil regions, where boom towns have gone bust, leaving behind a shattered industry that needs billions to reboot.

item.title: The RBA rate hike that will do nothing to curb inflation
item.description: As Reserve Bank board members gather for the second day of meetings this morning, they already would have been peppered with dire warnings about the potential impact of the energy shock.

item.title: Armed drones are cheap, easy to make and deadly — and they're changing the shape of conflicts
item.description: Defence analysts say the early lessons of the Iran conflict are fairly clear: drone warfare is here to stay and Australia needs to rapidly improve its counter-drone capabilities.

item.title: Employers reveal excluding candidates due to mental illness, disability and age
item.description: Seven in 10 employers reveal they are excluding candidates in certain groups, including those who live with a long-term illness, disability, mental illness or are over 55, according to a new survey.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
item.description: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

item.title: NSW government to introduce new laws to combat LGBTQIA+ hate crimes
item.description: Offenders convicted of hate crimes against the LGBTQIA+ community in New South Wales could face up to seven years behind bars under new legislation being introduced to parliament.

item.title: Israel says two top Iranian officials killed in air strikes in blow to Tehran leadership — as it happened
item.description: Israel says it has killed Iran's security chief Ali Larijani, one of the country's most senior officials. Read how events unfolded.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: What time do the Matildas play? How to watch and follow Australia vs China
item.description: The Matildas are in the final four at the Women's Asian Cup, and take on the defending champion China in the first semifinal tonight. Here's how to follow the game at Perth Stadium.

item.title: Young people particularly vulnerable to cults post-COVID
item.description: According to one of Australia's leading cult experts, the number of young adults between 17 and 25 years old joining cults since COVID is increasing at an alarming rate.

item.title: Deadline arrives for Kyle Sandilands to respond to misconduct claims after Jackie O tirade
item.description: Almost one month on from the explosive argument between its star hosts, the Kyle and Jackie O Show is expected to learn its fate on Tuesday.

item.title: Victorian duck hunters warned about littering ahead of 2026 season
item.description: Duck hunters are being warned not to leave rubbish behind ahead of the 2026 hunting season, with a duck rescue group saying more needs to be done about it.

item.title: Graziers fork out thousands of dollars at Jerilderie Working Dog Auction
item.description: The average price for a working dog at a recent auction in southern NSW was $9,500, but trainers insist they are a good investment.

item.title: Energy minister says panic buying is 'very much' behind fuel shortages — as it happened
item.description: Energy Minister Chris Bowen says panic buying is "very much" the cause of fuel shortages in Australia, particularly in regional communities.

item.title: Australian coach and former player Marinko Matosevic cops doping ban after admitting to blood transfusion
item.description: Former world number 39 Marinko Matosevic receives a four-year doping ban from tennis's global integrity agency, which he had labelled "corrupt" during its investigation.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle expected to bring severe impacts to Far North Queensland
item.description: The tropical low in the Coral Sea is now officially a cyclone, with the system expected to bring destructive winds and heavy rain when it reaches the coast.

item.title: RBA hikes cash rate to 4.1 per cent in a split decision — as it happened
item.description: The Reserve Bank has raised the cash rate to 4.1 per cent, its second hike in as many months as inflationary pressures continue.

item.title: Chelsea receives record Premier League fine for historical breaches of financial rules
item.description: Chelsea are fined a record total of $20.2 million for historical breaches of the Premier League's financial regulations, but avoid a points deduction.

item.title: Timothée Chalamet charms cinema-goers but can't crack the Academy
item.description: At the beginning of 2025, Timothée Chalamet was the hot pick for the Best Actor Oscar for his turn in Marty Supreme. But a lot can change in a few months.

item.title: Cancelled 2026 Commonwealth Games leaves unfinished legacy across Victoria
item.description: The 2026 Victorian Commonwealth Games would have started today, but attention now turns to the unbuilt housing and unfinished regional projects that were promised.

item.title: Monday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Cheaper, cleaner copper technology offers a lifeline as Mount Isa weighs uncertain future
item.description: Copper production in outback Queensland is facing a ticking clock, but researchers say a new refining process developed at the University of Queensland could slash production costs.

item.title: Body of French national believed to have jumped from a boat at Hobart dock found
item.description: Police say the 23-year-old man is thought to have jumped from a scientific vessel docked at Hobart's waterfront, as early indications show alcohol may have been involved.

item.title: Only private midwifery practice in WA's Kimberley shuts, leaving mothers with limited options
item.description: The only private midwifery practice in Broome is being forced to close because it is no longer sustainable to keep operations going.

item.title: Two men accused of raping woman who believed they were Uber drivers
item.description: Two men are on trial in the Northern Territory Supreme Court, accused of sexually assaulting a young woman in her apartment for eight hours after picking her up on a night out while she was waiting for an Uber.

item.title: Russian 'shadow fleet' gas tanker adrift in Mediterranean poses 'imminent' threat
item.description: Italy, France and seven other nations say a Russian liquefied natural gas tanker adrift in the Mediterranean represents an ecological threat, urging swift action.

item.title: Varroa mite investigation finds illegal imports unlikely but fails to find source of deadly incursion
item.description: Australia's leading biosecurity experts have been unable to trace the source of the of the bee-killing varroa mite incursion which has changed the face of beekeeping and pollination in Australia.

item.title: Why I'm taking a break from my marriage — in the backyard
item.description: After almost five decades of marriage, I needed a break. And since enjoying my new-found freedom, I'm not sure I'll ever go back.

item.title: WA farmer plans 2027 run across Canada to raise rural mental health awareness
item.description: A Wheatbelt farmer will attempt to run the entire length of Canada next year, aiming to cover 6,500 kilometres in less than 150 days.

item.title: In UAE, life goes on amid Iran's attacks but the future is uncertain
item.description: Amid Iran's missile and drone attacks, a weird sense of normality in Dubai and Abu Dhabi sits awkwardly with the news coverage of burning buildings and reports of strikes on airports.

item.title: Rights group says El Salvador forcibly disappearing nationals deported from the US
item.description: El Salvador has subjected some nationals deported from the US to enforced disappearance and arbitrary detention without revealing their whereabouts or bringing them before a judge.

item.title: China unveils next round of green energy ambitions in five-year plan
item.description: China is adopting green energy at a scale hard to imagine for many other countries, with the country's latest five-year plan further signalling its intention to reduce its dependency on foreign oil.

item.title: Petrol price rise amid war in the Middle East adds to cost-of-living focus in SA state election
item.description: Increased costs for petrol, food and housing are front and centre when it comes to how families are choosing to vote in the upcoming South Australian state election.

item.title: Bluesfest collapse leaves merchandise supplier $65k out of pocket
item.description: The merchandise suppliers were due to deliver more than 6,000 T-shirts and other branded items this week to be sold at the famous Byron Bay festival over Easter.

item.title: Northern Territory facing 'back-to-back' disasters as cyclone threat follows floods
item.description: Territorians are being urged to "get ahead" of a cyclone that could arrive in the NT this weekend, as the government announces a $100 million Flood Recovery Fund.

item.title: Eurogliders founder Bernie Lynch dies age 72
item.description: Australian music icon Bernie Lynch wrote all of Eurogliders's biggest songs, including the smash hit Heaven (Must Be There).

item.title: Cliff collapse at Port Willunga prompts Adelaide council warning
item.description: A South Australian mayor says no-one was hurt when boulders fell from a cliff face at a popular beach in Adelaide's south.

item.title: Diesel thefts rise in regional NSW as prices soar amid Middle East war
item.description: The NSW Police Rural Crime Team is reporting a spike in diesel being stolen from landholders amid soaring prices.

item.title: Scientists say summer of weather whiplash points to driving force of climate change
item.description: Scientists warn sharp swings from extreme heat to flooding this summer signal climate change is overtaking other weather drivers.

item.title: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of deadly air strike on drug rehab centre in Kabul
item.description: There are fears of heavy casualties as Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of hitting a treatment centre for drug addicts in the capital, Kabul, and killing civilians.

item.title: Fuel crisis forces WA resources company Blue Cap Mining to send workers home
item.description: Fuel shortages linked to the Middle East war are forcing a WA gold miner to stand down workers, exposing how smaller operators are being squeezed while major miners maintain supply.

item.title: Letters to hit-and-run victim's family 'vital' in Albany cold case investigation
item.description: WA detectives have travelled to the Great Southern city of Albany to re-investigate the hit-and-run death of Mark Edward Gregory in 1991.

item.title: RBA lifts interest rates by 0.25pc for second time this year
item.description: The Reserve Bank has lifted interest rates by 0.25 percentage points as the war in the Middle East sends oil, gas, and petrol prices soaring.

item.title: Izak Rankine to return for Crows after AFL homophobic slur ban, Swans back Charlie Curnow
item.description: Izak Rankine is set to play his first AFL match in 2026 since serving a four-match ban for a homophobic slur.

item.title: MAGA's civil war over Donald Trump's conflict in Iran descends into personal attacks
item.description: A civil war within the MAGA movement is getting more lewd by the day as prominent right-wing figures feud online over the war in Iran.

item.title: Cadia goldmine pollution allegations lead to class action against Newmont
item.description: A small farming community in central west NSW is taking on mining giant Newmont, alleging a goldmine at Cadia, near Orange, has contaminated their agricultural assets.

item.title: Identity of street artist Banksy revealed in new investigation
item.description: An in-depth investigation drawing on court files, photos and public records reports the world-famous anonymous artist is a Bristol-born man who later adopted a new identity.

item.title: Ipswich woman charged with murder of Katherine Sanowski
item.description: A 34-year-old woman has been charged over the alleged murder of Springfield woman Katherine Sanowski, who was found in an Ipswich home last month.

item.title: Mortgage repayment calculator: How much extra you could be paying now the cash rate is 4.1 per cent
item.description: Tell us your loan size, term and interest rate, and our mortgage repayment calculator will work out how much extra you may have to pay on your loan.

item.title: US becoming dominant military force in Northern Territory, report says
item.description: Defence analysts have criticised planning around the Northern Territory's defence infrastructure, saying Australia risks becoming the secondary user of the NT's geographic advantages, behind the US military.

item.title: Manildra ordered to pay $170,000 after silos spill grain into Shoalhaven River
item.description: The Environment Protection Authority has ordered Manildra to spend $170,000 on community projects after its grain silos collapsed into the Shoalhaven River almost two years ago.

item.title: Economists call for budget bravery as rising inflation contributes to another RBA rate rise
item.description: Economist are urging the government make brave budget calls as Australian households prepare to sweat through another rate rise.

item.title: Federation Council to remove Indigenous flags despite overwhelming community support
item.description: Despite strong opposition, the council on the NSW-Victoria border will remove Indigenous flags from its chamber after it was revealed there were no protocols to display them.

item.title: What is vaginismus and how do you know if you have it?
item.description: Madeleine Edwards had never heard of the term vaginismus before she was diagnosed with the condition.

item.title: Aerial vision of Mooloolah River Interchange
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Navigation sounders turned off before Nuyina hit ocean floor, ATSB report notes
item.description: A preliminary report has revealed new details about how an Australian research vessel hit the ocean floor after navigational echo sounders were turned off to stop interference with scientific sampling.

item.title: Ambassador to UAE on how embassy has dealt with Iran attacks
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Man who killed Perth lawyer Alice McShera at Crown Towers sentenced to 19 years in jail
item.description: Cameron Pearson, who pleaded guilty to killing Perth barrister Alice McShera by bashing her in the head with a champagne bottle, was sentenced to 19 years in jail.

item.title: Former Iranian footballer fears for athletes' safety as they return to Iran
item.description: Atefe Moradi knows Iranian regime's playbook well. She played for her home country's national league before moving to Australia, and fears for the safety of the female footballers returning to Iran.

item.title: Major banks announce interest rate increase after Reserve Bank lifts cash rate by 0.25 per cent
item.description: Australia's four major banks respond to the Reserve Bank of Australia's decision to increase the national cash rate to 4.1 per cent.

item.title: What are the best ways to save fuel?
item.description: From fuel-efficient driving to alternative travel modes, we look at practical ways to combat fuel shortages and price hikes.

item.title: Iranian duo join Brisbane Roar's W-League training side
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Bunbury masseur Anthony Brain sentenced to more than 15 years jail for raping clients
item.description: A masseur who described himself as a nudist is jailed for 15-and-a-half years for sexually assaulting more than a dozen female clients.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands says he hopes ARN Media contract will be honoured
item.description: Radio broadcaster Kyle Sandilands says he is considering "many options" after being taken off-air for insulting his co-host and friend of almost 30 years Jackie "O" Henderson.

item.title: 'Butchers, bakers and mechanics' among the Ukrainian civilians trained by Australians for the Russia war
item.description: Rather than depicting the front line, the Australian War Memorial's new exhibition focuses on preparation for war, the people caught in between and the Australians assisting them.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle tracker map: When will it hit the Queensland coast?
item.description: See the latest forecast and wind speeds as the system approaches Far North Queensland.

item.title: Man charged with murder of woman and baby in Belivah
item.description: A woman and a baby were found dead inside their Belivah home on Thursday morning when police attended the house to advise that the man had been hit by a car.

item.title: Tuesday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Defence minister confirms US can access data collected by Australian surveillance plane in Gulf
item.description: The defence minister has confirmed intelligence gathered by an Australian surveillance aircraft deployed to support Gulf states can be accessed by the United States, potentially informing its strikes on Iran.

item.title: Matildas book place in Asian Cup final with lion-hearted win over China
item.description: Goals from Caitlin Foord and Sam Kerr fire the Matildas to a 2-1 semifinal victory over China and a shot at a first continental crown since 2010.

item.title: Australia's Israeli embassy criticises Grace Tame's comments on Hamas October 7 attacks
item.description: Australia's Israeli embassy has criticised comments Grace Tame made about reports of sexual violence perpetrated by terrorist group Hamas.

item.title: Israel's invasion of Lebanon is pushing a fragile nation to its 'tipping point'
item.description: Israel is moving more troops into southern Lebanon ahead of what some fear will be a long occupation, and experts warn further destabilisation could threaten the country's existence.

item.title: IVF embryo mix-up discovered 30 years later
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Israel moves troops into Lebanon
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Sri Lanka introduces four-day work week to conserve fuel amid Middle East war
item.description: Sri Lanka has introduced a shorter work week for people to use less fuel amid the ongoing Middle East war.

item.title: Israel claims it has killed Iran's security chief Ali Larijani. Who was he?
item.description: When Israeli and US strikes killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at the start of the Middle East war, Iran's security chief Ali Larijani became even more powerful than he had been for decades.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Sam Kerr provides geometric genius as Katrina Gorry takes a battering — Matildas vs China quick hits
item.description: Sam Kerr gets her angles right, Katrina Gorry stands tall despite a battering and Kaitlyn Torpey has a birthday to remember. Here are the quick hits from the Matildas' exhilirating 2-1 semifinal triumph over China.

item.title: Historical IVF mix-up discovery raises questions about Australian industry
item.description: Fertility experts say the discovery of an IVF mix-up at Royal North Shore Hospital in 1995 could be the tip of the iceberg as more Australians research their parentage.

item.title: Travel quiz, March 18: Do you have the frequent flyers points to pass our test?
item.description: Which country’s currency is called the quetzal? And what is Thailand's Songkran festival best known for?

item.title: Behind Melbourne Museum's shiny facade sits a world hidden from view
item.description: Museums display only a fraction of their collection so we went behind the scenes at the Melbourne Museum to find the untold stories kept from public view.

item.title: Do Not Pass Go, a new dark comedy from Melbourne Theatre Company, might inspire you to quit your job
item.description: This dark comedy takes aim at capitalism and corporate work culture, but it also finds hope in the relationships between everyday people.

item.title: Middle East war forces pharmaceutical companies to reroute critical medicines to ensure Australian supply
item.description: The Middle East war is forcing major pharmaceutical companies to divert critical medicines away from one of the world's most important trade routes, to ensure there are no supply issues in Australia.

item.title: Reserve Bank governor warns of the recession we might have to have after interest rate hike
item.description: Is the RBA fighting last year's inflation war with its latest interest rate hike? Whatever the case may be, Australians are bracing for even more economic pain.

item.title: Labor hasn't got a capital gains tax policy yet, but it's already making the case
item.description: A Senate committee report into the capital gains tax was authored by the Greens but littered with clues about Labor's plans.

item.title: VIQ Solutions Australia placed into voluntary administration, court transcriptions services in limbo
item.description: Four weeks after the ABC revealed sensitive court files were being accessed offshore in a potential risk to national security, the company responsible for the breach has entered into voluntary administration.

item.title: Apartment height limits locked in across 25 Melbourne activity centres
item.description: The Victorian government has released its final planning controls for 25 activity centres in Melbourne, with tweaks meaning lower height limits in parts of Kew and Oakleigh and higher heights in parts of Coburg and Brunswick.

item.title: Court finds Japanese sterilisation law 'lacks rationality' and should be debated
item.description: A law in Japan bans childless women from accessing voluntary sterilisation. Now a Tokyo judge has found it "lacks rationality" and should be debated.

item.title: Counterfeit cash seizures up 700 per cent amid influx in fake currency from Asia
item.description: Cash may have declined in use during COVID but the use of it has steadied since. But technology has also opened a door to fake cash and its being seized now at much higher levels.

item.title: Cancer nurse banned for stealing medication from Royal Adelaide Hospital
item.description: A registered nurse has been banned for stealing opioid medication while working on an oncology ward at the Royal Adelaide Hospital, but a tribunal has allowed her to return to practise with strict conditions.

item.title: CSIRO survey finds drop in community support for Tasmanian salmon industry
item.description: A CSIRO report has found Tasmania's salmon farming industry has experienced a "significant and sustained shift" away from strong community support it experienced a decade ago.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle could be the first storm in 21 years to make landfall three times
item.description: The last Australian triple landfall system was Severe Tropical Cyclone Ingrid more than two decades ago.

item.title: In war-weary Lebanon, anger at Israel and Hezbollah takes hold
item.description: As Israel's intense bombardment continues into a third week, Lebanon's war-weary population and cash-strapped government are wondering when the fighting will stop.

item.title: Medical advocacy is crucial for young women but it can feel like a 'catch-22'
item.description: Young women have detailed their experiences being dismissed and misdiagnosed as chronic pain sufferers. Now, they're teaching others how to advocate for their health.

item.title: Petrol price spiral could put mobile early childcare education services at risk
item.description: Mobile services travelling across regional New South Wales say rising petrol prices are putting early childcare education at risk.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matthew Doran
item.description: The ABC's Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran is in Beirut and is providing daily updates on the Middle East war.

item.title: Live: Trump says US 'no longer' needs NATO allies, Australia in Iran war
item.description: Donald Trump says the US "do not need the help of anyone" in the war against Iran, calling out NATO allies and Australia in a fiery social media post. Follow live.

item.title: NSW government paid $700 to get triple killer Regina Arthurell out of foster home
item.description: Families and Communities Minister Kate Washington was grilled in parliament over the case of triple killer Regina Arthurell, who was living with two foster children in a Western Sydney home.

item.title: Iran's communications blackout leaves family in Australia in the dark
item.description: Iranian Australians with family in Iran are concerned for their loved ones, with internet and phone blackouts making it near-impossible to stay in contact.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Matildas' butterfly effect on display in win over China, but their evolution isn't complete
item.description: Sam Kerr has once again shown that she can single-handedly alter the course of Australian football history, but the rest of the team must follow after winning through to the Asian Cup final.

item.title: Taxpayer-funded bill for government staffers jumps almost 20 per cent in a year
item.description: The latest expense report shows the cost of salaries for the Queensland premier and ministers' staff topped $22 million.

item.title: One Nation is tipped to be a major player in the South Australian election
item.description: One Nation South Australia candidate Rocco DeAngelis says people are "disappointed" in the major parties ahead of the state election, but Greens SA leader Robert Simms says the party is "scapegoating minority groups".

item.title: Multiple demolition derbies cancelled after SafeWork NSW rules barriers unsafe
item.description: Agriculture show societies say late, inconsistent rulings by SafeWork NSW about safety precautions for demolition derbies have cost them thousands in attendance and ticket sales.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle to make landfall on Far North Queensland coast
item.description: A third severe weather system in less than two months is heading for Far North Queensland and set to bring wind gusts of up to 200 kilometres per hour.

item.title: Scott Pendlebury avoids first ever suspension at AFL tribunal due to 'exceptional circumstances'
item.description: Collingwood great Scott Pendlebury avoids suspension despite being found guilty of rough conduct at the AFL tribunal.

item.title: Live: Andrew Hastie labels Trump's outburst at allies, Australia 'petulant'
item.description: Liberal frontbencher Andrew Hastie has labelled the US president's outburst at allies as "petulant" and says that the rules-based order is "dead". Follow live.

item.title: Winter grain crop under pressure as fuel and fertiliser shortage hits at crunch time
item.description: Farmers are warning consumers to expect price rises in the supermarket as fuel and fertiliser shortages affect crop production and transport.

item.title: Lower Limestone Coast locals call for additional rescue services after fatal boating incident
item.description: A triple fatal boating incident off South Australia's Limestone Coast has led to renewed calls for a volunteer marine rescue service in the region's south.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands says ARN Media has terminated his $100m radio contract
item.description: Kyle Sandilands has announced he has been sacked by ARN Media after an on-air row with co-host Jackie Henderson last month.

item.title: Flood-affected NT residents 'very nervous' about potential impact of Tropical Cyclone Narelle
item.description: Residents facing a mammoth clean-up after devastating flooding across the Top End are now being urged to prepare for more rain and a possible cyclone in the days ahead.

item.title: Who is Anna Lapwood, the content creator bringing organ music to millions on social media
item.description: Anna Lapwood uses social media to share her love of the organ and favourite music from Bach to Lord of the Rings. Millions follow her and thousands more come to her concerts.

item.title: Live: Chance of RBA interest rate hike in May drops to 40pc, ASX likely to rise
item.description: The likelihood of a follow-up interest rate hike in May may has fallen substantially after the Reserve Bank's 5-4 vote in favour of yesterday's rate increase — the slimmest of majorities. Follow live.

item.title: Search for missing kayaker off Middle Beach north of Adelaide
item.description: Relatives of the man, aged in his 70s, rang police with concerns just before 11pm on Tuesday after he failed to return from Middle Beach, 40 kilometres away from Adelaide.

item.title: Matildas coach Joe Montemurro explains Hayley Raso absence in Asian Cup semifinal win over China
item.description: Matildas coach Joe Montemurro explains why star winger Hayley Raso was an unused substitute in the 2-1 semifinal win over China.

item.title: From plastics to explosives, the oil surge is set to spike prices well beyond the fuel pump
item.description: From mining to agriculture, higher oil prices from the war in the Middle East are not only increasing fuel costs, but prices for key chemicals as well. Can businesses cope?

item.title: Adelaide 36ers into NBL grand final after Bryce Cotton masterclass
item.description: To chants of "MVP", Bryce Cotton scored 38 points to steer the Adelaide 36ers past South East Melbourne and into their first grand final since 2018.

item.title: Police praise bravery of women who spoke out against Bunbury rapist masseur Anthony Brain
item.description: Detectives praise the bravery of more than a dozen women who testified against Bunbury massage therapist and rapist Anthony Brain, including Anwen Handmer.

item.title: Trump calls out Australia in blistering post declaring the US does not 'need' ally support in Iran
item.description: The United States president has declared America does "not need the help of anyone" in the war he launched against Iran, days after he called on allies to help reclaim a key shipping route.

item.title: Two men charged over fatal Bruce Highway crash in Burpengary
item.description: An allegedly stolen truck travelling the wrong way on the highway killed a man on his way home from work last month.

item.title: Police appeal for information into murder-kidnapping
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police release new footage in appeal over grandfather Chris Baghsarian's murder
item.description: Police have released new video showing three men they believe were involved in the mistaken identity kidnapping and murder of Sydney grandfather Chris Baghsarian last month.

item.title: Senegal stripped of AFCON title, Morocco declared winner months after controversial final
item.description: Senegal's 1-0 win in the Africa Cup of Nations final has been overturned by an appeals board and a 3-0 victory awarded to Morocco after Senegalese players left the pitch in protest during the controversial January clash.
